theater_description: Rapid regime-collapse phase spanning several urban nodes and corridors as Taliban control unraveled under U.S.-led intervention and Northern Alliance offensives.
geometry_method_note: Would require a moving-front geometry or multiple city-corridor sub-episodes rather than one fixed polygon.
military_area_note: National and subnational forces overlapped; many armed actors were mobile and not all national forces were continuously present in any one urban node.
civilian_area_note: Urban populations and road-corridor populations changed quickly as fronts collapsed and people fled.
split_reason: Belligerent structure, theater geometry, and violence mode changed rapidly; a single national denominator would be misleading.
quality_note: Plausible candidate episode, but a defensible denominator would require additional sub-episode work.

episode_name: Operation Moshtarak clear phase (Marjah–Nad Ali / central Helmand)
theater_description: Initial clear phase of Operation Moshtarak centered on Marjah and adjacent Nad Ali terrain inside the upper central Helmand River Valley.
geometry_method_note: Use Marjah plus immediately contiguous Nad Ali clear-phase terrain, not all Helmand and not the broader 15,000-force central-Helmand support belt.
military_area_note: Military denominator should count core ISAF/ANSF assault forces directly assigned to the Marjah–Nad Ali clear phase plus Taliban fighters in the town.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator should count civilians physically present in Marjah–Nad Ali during the clear phase, reduced for displacement and inability to leave mined areas.
split_reason: This episode has a coherent subnational geometry, a time-bounded clear phase, and relatively strong force and civilian reporting.
quality_note: Good triangulation on civilian deaths, force size, and displacement, but Afghan-security fatality counts are incomplete.

theater_description: Taliban seizure of Kunduz city followed by government counterattack and urban fighting, with casualty reporting available for Kunduz province over a city-centered episode window.
geometry_method_note: Best matched as Kunduz city plus immediate contested approaches; the civilian numerator comes from province-wide reporting, so the denominator is a city-plus-periphery scenario rather than city-only.
military_area_note: Military denominator brackets nominal city defenders and insurgent attackers against lower estimates of actual fighters who stood and fought.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator uses city-population and displacement-based scenarios rather than the whole province.
split_reason: Urban battle with distinct dates, strong civilian casualty reporting, and defensible city-centered denominator scenarios.
quality_note: Civilian counts are strong, but military-death totals and denominator matching are imperfect.

theater_description: Multi-day urban battle around Ghazni city with large civilian harm and substantial but disputed combatant losses.
geometry_method_note: Would require a Ghazni city polygon with road-access corridors and short-duration displacement adjustment.
military_area_note: Would need city defenders, reinforcements, and insurgent assault-force reconstruction.
civilian_area_note: Would need city population present during fighting rather than provincial totals.
split_reason: Urban battle with a compact timeline and potentially strong numerator sources, but not fully developed here.
quality_note: Promising candidate for future work; numerator coverage is likely decent, denominator work remains unfinished.

episode_name: Abbey Gate bombing at Hamid Karzai International Airport
theater_description: One-day suicide-bombing episode at the Abbey Gate corridor during the Kabul evacuation endgame.
geometry_method_note: Use the Abbey Gate queueing corridor and adjacent canal approaches on 2021-08-26, not all Kabul and not all airport grounds.
military_area_note: Military denominator counts U.S. troops and Afghan security personnel directly involved in airport security; Taliban outer-perimeter forces are not added because footprint-specific sourcing is insufficient.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator is the point-in-time exposed crowd in the Abbey Gate corridor, modeled from throughput, crowding, and late-evacuation reporting.
split_reason: The endgame phase is qualitatively different from the wider war, and the bombing is one of the few episodes with highly specific direct-death counts.
quality_note: Civilian and U.S. death counts are strong, but civilian exposure is heavily model-generated.
